title: cicuta bulbifera
intro: Cicuta bulbifera, also known as the Bulbous Water Hemlock, is a highly toxic and invasive plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). While it has no significant medicinal properties due to its toxicity, it has been used in some herbal preparations, albeit with extreme caution, as a treatment for certain conditions, such as epilepsy and rheumatism. The plant is believed to have potential health benefits, although its toxicity makes it unreliable for human consumption. However, herbal preparations like tinctures and infusions are sometimes used in traditional medicine, but only under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
